candidates.evidence_quote: I want to revisit how you're thinking about recapturing restaurant margins. As we think of 2024, what are you embedding for restaurant margin relative to pre-COVID and any puts and takes we should consider?

when it comes to menu price increases in 2024 and what you guys are contemplating, how are you looking at the tradeoff between either protecting or growing margin and any potential traffic headwinds that could come with that pricing?
so that's an area that cheesecake factory best practices and systems have been deployed over time to North Italia, and we see a really good opportunity to lower food costs over time
certainly we're lapping really heightened commodity inflation there too as well
The inflation for wages are going to be low to mid single digits, and the inflation for commodities are low single digits, right?
beef remains a little bit challenging, some ups and downs recently, but, you know, definitely a pressured headwind going into next year
Certainly the labor environment is promising today. And I think our brand reputation helps quite a bit with that and our ability to attract and retain employers.

Wanted to talk about unit growth and if you could provide some more feedback on how to look at store closures to net against the new unit openings for the 7% growth rate you mentioned for 2024 and then the 16 new units for 2023.

The second one that we're focused on is really bringing new restaurants up to speed over time a little bit more faster. The mature margin is one thing, but if you can bring the early stages of the restaurants up faster, it sets up for success in the longer term as well.
Could you provide a little bit more detail on the same-store sales trend for North Italia, breaking it out by traffic and mix and pricing going into fourth quarters?
I think that it does imply that we think we'll be in positive territory for comps and we think there could be some leverage opportunity
I think we've seen better than industry traffic. It feels like a relatively normal year.
what was the off-premise sales mix at Cheesecake Factory in the quarter?
And I think that's why we launch rewards, right, as a means to drive incremental visitation.

can you shed some light on what the tax rate assumption is in that range
We target getting a little bit of leverage. off of GNA as well.
We are on track with Flower Child, bringing it fully under our umbrella for this year.

We are on track with Flower Child, bringing it fully under our umbrella for this year. We're looking forward to accelerating the pace of openings next year. We would anticipate over time that it too would be a 20% grower year over year.
we are on track to get to the margin target that we need to be at to make it a growth vehicle, which is in that mid-teens level. So we feel really good overall about the trajectory of Flower Child.
